figures.caption: Fig. 35–1. An atomic system with spin jj has (2j+1)(2j+1) possible energy values in a magnetic field B\FigB. The energy splitting is proportional to BB for small fields.
Fig. 35–2. The experiment of Stern and Gerlach.
Fig. 35–3. The classical precession of an atom with the magnetic moment μ\Figmu and the angular momentum J\FigJ.
Fig. 35–4. The angle of precession of an atomic magnet can be changed by a horizontal magnetic field always at right angles to μ\Figmu, as in (a), or by an oscillating field, as in (b).
Fig. 35–6. The current of atoms in the beam decreases when ω=ωp\omega=\omega_p.
Fig. 35–7. The variation of the paramagnetic magnetization with the magnetic field strength BB.
Fig. 35–8. A nuclear magnetic resonance apparatus.
